mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and supports equity multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often after FEDFUNDS declines over rolling 3-month windows, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above the recent level signals weakening labor demand and higher recession risk, which depresses risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate is indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in RSAFS and HOUST identifies broad household demand and housing turnover, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and HOUST does not improve 60-day forward returns versus either series alone, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO reflects weaker industrial production and softer real activity, which is a negative earnings-growth signal for equities and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly drops in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the sign of the association flips in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates are compressed and policy stays behind inflation, which supports equities over the next 60 trading days through easier financial conditions.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.5% while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-policy gap does not coincide with positive 60-day excess returns, or if positive returns occur less than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend indicates improving payroll creation and stronger near-term income growth, which supports consumption and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS changes fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns across rolling samples, or if the short-horizon hit rate falls below 52% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combined decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies demand contraction across output and spending, which should reduce expected corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts on the latest quarter and RSAFS is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint contraction signal does not lead to lower 60-day returns than baseline, or if the interaction loses significance after accounting for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment, low-housing-start environment, weak HOUST together with elevated UNRATE signals broad slack and earnings pressure, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 5% and HOUST is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slack condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered observations yield positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases.
